contentBlocks.original:
ॐ नमः प्रणवार्थाय शुद्धज्ञानैकमूर्तये
निर्मलाय प्रशान्ताय दक्षिणामूर्तये नमः
contentBlocks.transliteration:
oṃ namaḥ praṇavārthāya śuddhajñānaikamūrtaye
nirmalāya praśāntāya dakṣiṇāmūrtaye namaḥ
contentBlocks.translation: Om, Salutations to Dakṣiṇāmūrti, the embodiment of the sacred syllable Om, the form of pure, undivided knowledge, free from impurity, tranquil, and serene.
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – Om; नमः (namaḥ) – salutations; प्रणवार्थाय (praṇavārthāya) – to the meaning of Om (praṇava:Om | arthāya:to the meaning); शुद्धज्ञानैकमूर्तये (śuddhajñānaikamūrtaye) – to the form of pure knowledge (śuddha:pure | jñāna:knowledge | eka:one | mūrtaye:to the form); निर्मलाय (nirmalāya) – to the spotless; प्रशान्ताय (praśāntāya) – to the tranquil; दक्षिणामूर्तये (dakṣiṇāmūrtaye) – to Dakṣiṇāmūrti (dakṣiṇa:south | mūrtaye:to the form); नमः (namaḥ) – salutations;
